_comment: D9 billing-model catalog. S5 (furnace/idle-burn) must ingest each box's provider BILLING MODEL and frame WASTED-TOKEN impact correctly: 'furnace' is about wasted tokens/usage in ALL billing models, not only money. Two billing types: (i) subscription/flat/allowance - extra tokens may cost no extra dollars but STILL consume session/weekly usage limits; (ii) pay-per-token/metered - extra tokens = direct money. The per-box binding (which provider is on which billing type) is resolved from the box's OWN provider config and pinned in baseline.json; this file is the generic catalog + framing templates. NO client names, NO secrets, NO account ids.

subscription_allowance.frame: Wasted tokens consume the session/weekly USAGE ALLOWANCE, not dollars. Burning the allowance risks throttling or a hard stop mid-task even though the invoice does not move.

pay_per_token_metered.frame: Wasted tokens are DIRECT MONEY - every idle/heartbeat token is billed. This is spend leaving the account with no work produced.

provider_billing_defaults.note: Ollama Cloud - flat/allowance. Extra tokens may cost no extra dollars but STILL burn session-usage and weekly-usage limits. The classic signed-in-daemon idle furnace.
OpenRouter - metered. Extra tokens = direct money.
Metered pay-per-token (never touched by the sentinel beyond read-only posture).

default_note: When a box's provider billing type cannot be resolved from its own config, assume metered (the conservative, money-framed case) and say so in the alert (posture: UNRESOLVED).

provider_check.note: D9 hybrid tail: the READ-ONLY provider usage/balance check runs ONLY when on-box detection flags idle burn or needs confirmation. It reports usage-consumed vs balance-remaining; it never mutates a provider account and never prints a credential value.
